title: Infinite genvar for-loop with always-true condition causes Icarus Verilog to hang indefinitely
body:
Design generated by Fuzzing.

module test;

  genvar i;
  generate
    // 无限递增，永不满足退出条件
    for (i = 0; i >= 0; i = i + 1) begin : infinite_block
      wire dummy;
    end
  endgenerate

endmodule

Iverilog-V
 Icarus Verilog Parser/Elaborator version 12/13.0 

Expected Behavior
A warning or error should be issued, or the compiler should safely detect the infinite loop and exit with a diagnostic message (as required by synthesis constraints).

Actual Behavior
Icarus Verilog freezes indefinitely when compiling this file:

bash
$ iverilog test.v
# (No output. Compiler hangs, CPU usage spikes, never completes)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d51efda2-0f91-4999-be43-ee568dd513bb)

This issue consumes CPU and memory indefinitely and can lead to resource exhaustion.
